string: System health: Database size, API response times (slower than 2 seconds may indicate a problem), and error rates (should be near zero).
line_text: System health: Database size, API response times (slower than 2 seconds may indicate a problem), and error rates (should be near zero).

string: • Admin: Full access including this admin panel, score weights, and user management.
line_text: • Admin: Full access including this admin panel, score weights, and user management.

string: s overall organizing potential, calculated from up to 8 different factors. The score only uses factors where we actually have data -- if we
line_text: Score (0-10): This employer's overall organizing potential, calculated from up to 8 different factors. The score only uses factors where we actually have data -- if we're missing information on a factor, it's skipped rather than counted against the employer. A score of 8.0 based on 7 factors is more reliable than an 8.0 based on 3 factors. The number of factors used is shown below the score.

string: • Strong (next 12%): Very promising targets with solid data across several factors. Action: worth detailed research and preliminary outreach assessment.
line_text: • Strong (next 12%): Very promising targets with solid data across several factors. Action: worth detailed research and preliminary outreach assessment.

string: Factor bars: Each bar shows how this employer scored on one of 8 factors, rated 0-10. Factors are weighted by importance -- (3x) factors matter three times as much as (1x) factors in the final score. A grayed-out factor with a dash means we have no data for that factor.
line_text: Factor bars: Each bar shows how this employer scored on one of 8 factors, rated 0-10. Factors are weighted by importance -- (3x) factors matter three times as much as (1x) factors in the final score. A grayed-out factor with a dash means we have no data for that factor.

string: • Union Proximity (3x): Whether companies in the same corporate family already have unions. Strongest predictor -- the corporate parent has already dealt with unions elsewhere.
line_text: • Union Proximity (3x): Whether companies in the same corporate family already have unions. Strongest predictor -- the corporate parent has already dealt with unions elsewhere.

string: • Employer Size (3x): Larger employers offer more impact per campaign. Employers under 15 employees score zero.
line_text: • Employer Size (3x): Larger employers offer more impact per campaign. Employers under 15 employees score zero.

string: • NLRB Activity (3x): Nearby union election momentum (within 25 miles, similar industry) and this employer's own election history. Past losses count as a negative.
line_text: • NLRB Activity (3x): Nearby union election momentum (within 25 miles, similar industry) and this employer's own election history. Past losses count as a negative.

string: • Gov Contracts (2x): Federal, state, or city government contracts create public accountability and regulatory leverage. Multiple contract levels score higher.
line_text: • Gov Contracts (2x): Federal, state, or city government contracts create public accountability and regulatory leverage. Multiple contract levels score higher.

string: • Industry Growth (2x): BLS-projected 10-year industry growth rate. Faster-growing industries mean more workers entering the field.
line_text: • Industry Growth (2x): BLS-projected 10-year industry growth rate. Faster-growing industries mean more workers entering the field.

string: • OSHA Safety (1x): Workplace safety violations. More violations and more serious violations (willful, repeat) score higher. Recent ones count more.
line_text: • OSHA Safety (1x): Workplace safety violations. More violations and more serious violations (willful, repeat) score higher. Recent ones count more.

string: • WHD Wage Theft (1x): Wage and hour violations including back wages, overtime, and minimum wage violations.
line_text: • WHD Wage Theft (1x): Wage and hour violations including back wages, overtime, and minimum wage violations.

string: • Financial (1x): Revenue scale, asset cushion, and revenue-per-worker from 990 filings or SEC data.
line_text: • Financial (1x): Revenue scale, asset cushion, and revenue-per-worker from 990 filings or SEC data.

string: • F-7: DOL Form LM-10/F-7 filings. Employers with union contracts are required to file these.
line_text: • F-7: DOL Form LM-10/F-7 filings. Employers with union contracts are required to file these.

string: • OSHA: Occupational Safety and Health Administration inspection records.
line_text: • OSHA: Occupational Safety and Health Administration inspection records.

string: • NLRB: National Labor Relations Board case records -- election petitions, results, and unfair labor practice complaints.
line_text: • NLRB: National Labor Relations Board case records -- election petitions, results, and unfair labor practice complaints.

string: • WHD: Wage and Hour Division enforcement records -- wage theft, overtime, minimum wage investigations.
line_text: • WHD: Wage and Hour Division enforcement records -- wage theft, overtime, minimum wage investigations.

string: Confidence dots: How confident the system is that records from a data source were correctly matched to this employer. 4 dots = matched on unique ID (EIN or exact name + address). 3 dots = name + state or city. 2 dots = fuzzy name similarity + location. 1 dot = name similarity alone -- treat with caution.
line_text: Confidence dots: How confident the system is that records from a data source were correctly matched to this employer. 4 dots = matched on unique ID (EIN or exact name + address). 3 dots = name + state or city. 2 dots = fuzzy name similarity + location. 1 dot = name similarity alone -- treat with caution.

string: Employee count range: Different databases collect employee counts at different times using different definitions. The platform shows the range across all sources so you can see the spread. The scoring system uses the average.
line_text: Employee count range: Different databases collect employee counts at different times using different definitions. The platform shows the range across all sources so you can see the spread. The scoring system uses the average.

line_text: • Employee size: Filter to employers within a size range (e.g. 100-500).

line_text: • Score tier: Show only employers in a specific tier (Priority, Strong, etc.).
